No user-value tradeoff, no unresolved CONFLICT, no contract-compromise risk.

planning.approval_evidence: Planning approved by user with Tier A `approve` after vision banner. 9 tasks created covering schema foundation, agent asset, routing surfaces, tests, documentation, and verification. Contract coverage validated (every AC1-11/SC1-4 has implementing or verifying task). adv_change_validate passed; only warning is NO_DELTAS (deltas captured in design.md draft section, not as change.deltas — same pattern as addDelegationMatrix parent).

execution.approval_evidence: Execution complete. All 9 tasks done. Final verification: focused designer suites 294 passed, pnpm run check (typecheck + test-isolation + lockfile-policy + lint + format:check) green. Touched: agent asset, Zod schema + tool description, specs (delegation-defaults + subagent-reports), command files (adv-prep, adv-apply, adv-review, adv-harden), roster/matrix/exclusion tests, documentation surfaces (ADV_INSTRUCTIONS, SETUP, README, project, AGENTS). Working tree clean after final prettier --write campsite fix on src/tools/gate.ts (pre-existing drift).

acceptance.approval_evidence: Acceptance complete. Independent adv-reviewer worker (phase review, attempt 1) returned READY verdict with no blockers, no issues, only praise across 12 dimensions; 344 focused tests pass; pnpm run check clean. Executive summary persisted. Contract review matrix persisted (35 rows: SC1-4 pass, AC1-11 pass, C1-6 respected, DONT1-7 respected, OOS1-7 respected). Awaiting Tier B sign-off before archive.

items.text: `adv-designer` is a real spawnable agent asset with explicit frontend/component ownership, backend exclusions, no nested delegation, and no `/adv-*` slash invocation.
ADV apply routing can structurally choose `adv-designer` vs `adv-engineer` for frontend, backend, and mixed UI/backend tasks.
Review/harden guidance keeps `adv-reviewer` as owner and supplies frontend/design skill/checklist context for design-inclusive review work.
Delegation and report correctness are enforced by specs, schemas, packet contracts, and tests rather than prompt-only prose.
`adv-designer` exists as real spawnable sub-agent asset with `mode: subagent`, no nested delegation, no `/adv-*` slash invocation, and ADV orchestration mutation tools blocked.
`adv-designer` is apply-phase only and write-only for scoped frontend/component implementation; it does not own review or harden gates.
Designer scope covers HTML/CSS/JS/TSX/component work, semantic HTML, accessibility, responsive behavior, visual polish, site-design consistency, finer details, and component correctness.
Mixed UI/backend work is split by concern: designer gets UI/component work; engineer gets backend/state/API/business logic.
Neighboring UI/design recommendations are surfaced to ADV/user for HITL instead of silently expanding scope.
`delegation-defaults` and `subagent-reports` specs are updated where affected; no prompt-only routing source of truth.
`adv-designer` has a strict task-scoped typed report contract if it writes code; missing packet identity anchors remain validation errors, not inferred values.
Prep/apply routing has structural frontend metadata or equivalent; title/path heuristics may assist but do not own correctness.
Tests fail if `adv-designer` is referenced without a real agent asset, valid spawn mode, allowed routing, packet contract, report schema, and deploy/sync coverage.
`addDelegationMatrix` contract is preserved: matrix is source/evaluation law; downstream field agents do not inspect repo-local spec during normal execution.
Reviews for work including frontend/design scope remain routed to `adv-reviewer` and include a frontend/design skill or checklist in the review packet/context.
Specs are laws: `delegation-defaults` and `subagent-reports` must be updated where affected before implementation claims conformance.

Follow `addDelegationMatrix` contract: matrix/spec is source-plane law and evaluation artifact; deployed command/agent guidance carries runtime instructions without requiring downstream field-agent spec lookup.
Follow `adv-agent-tool-contracts`: schema, context packet, prompt, transport lane, tests, specs all aligned.
`adv-designer` must use apply-phase worker boundaries like `adv-engineer`: no nested delegation, no ADV orchestration mutations, scoped workdir, typed report submission if persisted.
Package commands run from `plugin/`.
Coordinate basis with `addDelegationMatrix` before implementation if parent remains unarchived or changes the same specs/tests.
Do not make `adv-designer` own backend logic, storage, APIs, Temporal, business rules, or non-UI implementation.
Do not make `adv-designer` a review/harden gate owner.
Do not route work to phantom/nonexistent agents or primary agents as sub-agents.
Do not duplicate delegation defaults as independent prompt prose across command files or agents.
Do not weaken typed report validation, packet identity anchors, gate ownership, human checkpoints, TDD evidence, worktree isolation, or ADV state mutation boundaries.
Do not rely on title/path heuristics as the sole authority for frontend routing correctness.
Do not silently expand a designer task into neighboring UI changes; surface recommendations to ADV/user for HITL.
Backend logic ownership by `adv-designer`.
Review/harden gate ownership by `adv-designer`.
Broad design-system rebuilds or visual redesigns unrelated to worker routing.
Product strategy or subjective UX direction beyond scoped task execution and recommendation surfacing.
Utility-command delegation matrix expansion unless design proves it is required for designer routing.
Changing global sub-agent nesting depth, max parallelism, or Task tool runtime guards.
Replacing `adv-engineer`, `adv-reviewer`, `adv-researcher`, `adv-tron`, `explore`, or `general`.

acceptanceCriteria: `adv-designer` exists as real spawnable sub-agent asset with `mode: subagent`, no nested delegation, no `/adv-*` slash invocation, and ADV orchestration mutation tools blocked.
`adv-designer` is apply-phase only and write-only for scoped frontend/component implementation; it does not own review or harden gates.
Designer scope covers HTML/CSS/JS/TSX/component work, semantic HTML, accessibility, responsive behavior, visual polish, site-design consistency, finer details, and component correctness.
Mixed UI/backend work is split by concern: designer gets UI/component work; engineer gets backend/state/API/business logic.
Neighboring UI/design recommendations are surfaced to ADV/user for HITL instead of silently expanding scope.
`delegation-defaults` and `subagent-reports` specs are updated where affected; no prompt-only routing source of truth.
`adv-designer` has a strict task-scoped typed report contract if it writes code; missing packet identity anchors remain validation errors, not inferred values.
Prep/apply routing has structural frontend metadata or equivalent; title/path heuristics may assist but do not own correctness.
Tests fail if `adv-designer` is referenced without a real agent asset, valid spawn mode, allowed routing, packet contract, report schema, and deploy/sync coverage.
`addDelegationMatrix` contract is preserved: matrix is source/evaluation law; downstream field agents do not inspect repo-local spec during normal execution.
Reviews for work including frontend/design scope remain routed to `adv-reviewer` and include a frontend/design skill or checklist in the review packet/context.

clarify_findings.message: Proposal references authentication/authorization without specifying the model. What auth mechanism will be used?
